reasoning: San Diego FC will enter MLS in 2025 as the 30th franchise. Under the current MLS playoff format, 9 out of 15 teams per conference (60%) make the playoffs. Looking at recent MLS expansion teams (since 2017), the inaugural-season performance is highly variable: St. Louis City (1st), LAFC (3rd), and Atlanta United (4th) succeeded immediately, while Austin FC (12th) and FC Cincinnati (12th) struggled. Applying a historical percentile to the 9-team qualification threshold suggests expansion teams have approximately a 40-50% chance of reaching the playoffs in year one. While San Diego has shown significant ambition—signing Hirving "Chucky" Lozano as a marquee Designated Player and integrating with the Right to Dream academy—building a roster from scratch carries inherent volatility and chemistry risks. Given the strong 60% base rate for any team to qualify but slightly discounting for the expansion penalty, a coin-flip 50% probability is a solidly calibrated estimate.
